sections.paragraph:
διαγίνομαι (s. γίνομαι) 2 aor. διεγενόμην to pass/elapse of time (Lysias, Or. 1, 15 χρόνου μεταξὺ διαγενομένου; X., Isaeus et al.; Joseph., s. below; PStras 41, 42 πολὺς χρόνος διαγέγονεν [250 a.d.]; POxy 68, 18; PLond V, 1676, 40; PFamTebt 15, 139; Mitt-Wilck. I/2, 41, col. III, 25; cp. διά A2a; LXX has the word only 2 Macc 11:26 in another mng.) διαγενομένου τοῦ σαββάτου when the Sabbath was over Mk 16:1. ἡμερῶν διαγενομένων τινῶν several days afterward Ac 25:13 (cp. Jos., Ant. 7, 394). ἱκανοῦ χρόνου διαγενομένου since or when considerable time had passed 27:9.—M-M.

aor. aor. = aorist

Lysias Lysias , V–IV b.c.—List 5

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Isaeus Isaeus , V–IV b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

PStras PStras = Griechische Papyrus der kaiserlichen Universitäts- und Landesbibliothek zu Strassburg—List 4

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

PLond PLond = PLondon=Greek Papyri in the British Museum—List 4

Mitt-Wilck. Mitt-Wilck. = LMitteis/UWilcken, Grundzüge und Chrestomathie der Papyruskunde—Lists 4, 6

col. col. = column

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

mng. mng. = meaning(s)

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 227
